steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The passage sets up a contradiction in nature that Dillard struggles to reconcile. The blank sentence presents the resolution: these opposing qualities are inextricably linked. The transition must signal this is the ultimate truth that answers her struggle.

steps.title: Match the signal
steps.content: ”Ultimately” is correct because it signals a final conclusion—”this is what it comes down to.” The other options either add information without showing resolution (Moreover), suggest a means to an end (To that end), or present a consequence (Hence) rather than the ultimate insight being offered. That matches choice A — A is correct.
